but what is point yaar???

His son/daughter will also grow up to be same tea seller, same small house, no electrical appliances or knowledge nothing.....

I feel bad for my Pakistani broz. they deserve better than just pale skin
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board today, bringing with it a torrent of insults and baiting.

The system intensified into a high-pressure front of aggression, fueled by Anon's claim that 'Abbos could never,' which sparked a flurry of hostile responses.

A scattered drizzle of genetics and cultural norms discussion fell across the board, with one anon remarking that 'Agar mughlo ne invade nahi kiya hota, toh tum logo ki maa yeh phen ke ghumti public mai kekekek.' This was followed by a brief but intense heat wave of Aryan supremacy claims, with another anon declaring 'soythies aasi abbos btfo
aryans win.' Meanwhile, a cold snap of beef-eating Hindu nationalism swept through the board, with an anon proclaiming a 'cult of patriotic indra worshippers' and boasting of 'shitting over ur puranic concept of veganism and caste poopremacy.' A weak front of piracy moved in, with a respondent seeking a torrent for the movie 'Bombay Velvet.' The overall climate remained overcast, with a drought of civil discourse persisting throughout the day.

articles.title: Anon Claims 'Abbos Could Never', Sparks Insults and Caste Baiting
articles.body:
Anon started the discussion with the statement 'Abbos could never'. One anon responded with 'teri maa saaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa', seemingly a personal attack. Another anon claimed to be 'text book definition of brown' and stated that others were inferring it wrong.

Anon engaged in caste baiting, asking 'your caste? i am baman but dark / nobody thinks I am brahmin until they see my name'. This led to more personal attacks and insults.

Anon joked about fonts, suggesting that others might have confusing sans serif fonts installed on their devices. This was in response to someone pointing out the difference between 'I' and 'l'.

The conversation involved several personal attacks, with one anon saying 'you retard / i said Ic / "i c" / Not LC / kabhi padhna seekh le, chutmarike'. Another anon accused someone of baiting and claimed it was working.

Anon used explicit language and made derogatory comments about others' mothers. For example, 'Suck toh teri ma karegi mera mota lund' and 'teri maa ko gubbara karde inchiye'.

The discussion also touched on reading and literacy, with one anon telling another to 'Ctrl+F if you can't read it pyre'. The conversation remained hostile and insulting throughout.

articles.title: Genetics and Cultural Norms
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion by remarking that if the Mughals hadn't invaded, Indians would be criticizing them for being promiscuous.

One respondent suggested that historical events like the Black Death and wars influenced the genetic makeup of European populations, contributing to what they described as high rates of infidelity and cuckoldry.

They claimed that men and women from elite backgrounds in France were known for their infidelity, which became a cultural norm over several centuries.

Another anon countered that while elites can shape culture, their influence is limited when they don't control the monopoly on violence.

They argued that in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), even if elite men were promiscuous, they managed to keep their women under control within their feudal systems.

A discussion on the relationship between genetics and culture ensued, with one participant suggesting that culture is downstream from genetics. However, another anon pointed out that this perspective can be seen as circular reasoning.

The conversation also touched on the historical context of modesty and dress codes in India. Some anons mentioned that before the Mughal invasion and British colonial rule, women in certain regions of India did not cover their breasts as a norm.

Others noted that the concept of veiling (ghunghat) and blouse were introduced later. The thread also saw exchanges on the idea that demographic changes, such as the replacement of monogamous individuals by more promiscuous people, contributed to the normalization of promiscuity in certain cultures.

Some participants referenced historical and regional variations in dress and behavior to support their arguments. One Himachali gunda argued that genetics play a significant role in shaping cultures and civilizations, citing the example of the Chinese script and language as being distinct from others.

Another anon questioned the extent to which the Black Death affected Europe compared to the Middle East and North Africa.

articles.title: Anon Claims 'Aryans Win' in Response to Unspecified Post
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with the statement 'soythies aasi abbos btfo aryans win.' Another anon responded by criticizing those who react negatively to others' posts without contributing to the conversation, stating that the point of an anonymous imageboard is to have fun and be a little crazy, not to care about what others think.

A respondent expressed confusion, comparing a Pakistani tea seller to someone from Uttar Pradesh with access to modern amenities, questioning who is 'winning' in terms of socio-economic status.

Anon countered that even if a Pakistani individual may have pale skin, their socio-economic conditions and opportunities for their children may not improve, eliciting sympathy for Pakistani individuals.

One participant made derogatory comments about Indian women, suggesting they want to be controlled and are drawn to Muslim men, who they believe can provide the thrill and masculinity they crave.

The conversation also involved discussions of racial and ethnic identities, with some anons making claims about their own and others' ancestry, and comparisons between different groups.

The thread included various exchanges of a critical and often hostile nature, with some anons questioning others' opinions and making aggressive remarks.

articles.title: Anon Claims Beef-Eating Hindus Will Reclaim Bharat
articles.body:
Anon posted about creating a cult of patriotic Indra worshippers who eat beef and shit over 'puranic concept of veganism and caste poopremacy.' They claimed beef-eating Hindus will invade Kashmir and reclaim Bharat.

Another anon replied that they eat beef if it's available, while a third anon stated that one can be non-Vedic and still be dharmic, citing East Asian Buddhists who eat beef.

Anon also joked about killing cows, with one respondent calling it 'cringe.'

articles.title: Bombay Velvet torrent hunt
articles.body: Anon sought to download the movie Bombay Velvet, having trouble finding a decent quality torrent on sites like rutracker, fmhy, and Reddit's piracy megathread. Another anon suggested checking The Pirate Bay, providing a search link and advising to open multiple results in a torrent client to wait for seeding. The discussion also touched on other piracy sites like YTS and Rarbg, with some anons reporting they are down.
